channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Proposed constitutional amendments in Zimbabwe could consolidate power and limit democratic processes
item.description: Zimbabwe’s ruling party, Zanu-PF, has introduced a bill proposing constitutional amendments that would extend presidential and parliamentary terms by two years. If passed, this would enable President ...

item.title: Ottawa Police Issue 36 Tickets in School Zone Safety Operation
item.description: Ottawa police conducted a safety blitz in school zones, issuing a total of 36 tickets to drivers who were found violating traffic rules. The operation aimed to enhance the safety of children and pedes...

item.title: Petrol and Diesel Prices Increased Amid Global Oil Price Surge
item.description: On Thursday, Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik announced an increase in petrol and high-speed diesel prices, setting petrol at Rs458.4 per litre and HSD at Rs520.35 per litre. He spoke at a joint p...

item.title: Pakistan emphasises peace and warns against escalation with India
item.description: Pakistani Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has issued a stern reminder to his Indian counterpart, Rajnath Singh, stating that the notion of war between two nuclear-armed nations is completely unthinkable...

item.title: SpaceX and OpenAI IPOs Likely Offer Limited Gains for Public Investors
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX and AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are expected to launch massive initial public offerings (IPOs) in 2026, potentially raising billions and marking high-profile market event...

item.title: Adekunle Gold and Simi Celebrate Arrival of Second Child
item.description: Nigerian music stars Adekunle Gold and Simi don welcome their second pikin as Simi announce am on Instagram. The baby wey dem born on March 10, 2026, come dey mark another happy chapter for the couple...

item.title: Understanding the Role of Pentecostal Churches in Daily Life in Southern Africa
item.description: A recent study on Pentecostal churches in southern Africa challenges the common perception that these churches are primarily sites of strange miracles or sensational acts. While media often highlights...

item.title: NIA to Investigate Incident Where Judicial Officers Were Detained During Voter List Revision in Malda
item.description: In West Bengal's Malda district, a serious incident occurred during the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. Seven judicial officers, including three women, were surrounded and...

item.title: Samburu Residents Protest Alleged Harassment of Governor Lati Lelelit by Senators
item.description: In Maralal Town, hundreds of Samburu residents staged a protest against senators who allegedly harassed Governor Lati Lelelit outside the Senate premises. The confrontation occurred when three senator...

item.title: Pakistan reiterates readiness to defend sovereignty amid Pahalgam attack anniversary
item.description: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif of Pakistan has emphasised the country's unwavering commitment to peace and regional stability while issuing a strong warning to India. Speaking in the context of the app...

item.title: Tanzania Explores Climate Finance Opportunities Through Project Preparation Initiatives
item.description: Dar es Salaam saw a high-level dialogue hosted by The Peninsula Club where leaders from the private sector, investors, and government officials discussed ways to unlock climate finance and boost Tanza...

item.title: Lori Loughlin Embraces Single Life and Casual Dating After Separation
item.description: Nearly six months after her separation from fashion designer Mossimo Giannulli, Lori Loughlin, the actress known for 'Full House' and 'When Calls the Heart,' is reportedly stepping back into dating. A...

item.title: Lili Reinhart addresses body-shaming comment made by director on set
item.description: Actress Lili Reinhart has shared a troubling experience in which a director made a comment about her physical appearance during filming. Speaking in a video interview with Cosmopolitan alongside her '...

item.title: Environmental Groups Question Nairobi National Park Parking Development
item.description: The Green Belt Movement has raised serious concerns over a planned parking facility near the Bomas International Convention Centre, which they say threatens Nairobi National Park. The lobby argues tha...

item.title: Metropolitan Economies Key to South Africa’s Growth and Employment
item.description: A new report from the Spatial Economic Activity Data - South Africa (SEAD-SA) partnership highlights that the performance of South Africa’s national economy is closely tied to the health of its metrop...

item.title: Durban Man Arrested for Impersonating SAPS Officer in Multiple Operations
item.description: A 39-year-old man from Glen Hills, Durban, has been arrested for posing as a high-ranking officer in the South African Police Service (SAPS) KwaZulu-Natal provincial office. Sthembiso Zondi assumed th...

item.title: Trump Addresses Nation on Progress in Iran Conflict Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: On Wednesday evening, US President Donald Trump delivered a televised address to the nation regarding the ongoing military actions in Iran. Trump stated that US forces had made significant progress, i...

item.title: Western Cape High Court blocks mortuary at Mowbray Muslim Cemetery
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has issued a final interdict preventing the construction of a mortuary at the historic Mowbray Muslim Cemetery. Judge Gayaat Da Silva Salie ruled that the Muslim Cemetery B...

item.title: Kisii Woman Struggling With Breast Cancer Seeks Support for Medical Treatment
item.description: Lydia Kerubo Omwenga, a 38-year-old mother from Keroka, Kisii County, is appealing for help as she battles breast cancer. She previously worked in the Gulf but had to return to Kenya due to financial ...

item.title: Retha Smit earns PhD at 67, highlighting lifelong learning and perseverance
item.description: At 67 years old, Dr Retha Smit has achieved a remarkable academic milestone by earning her PhD in psychiatry from Stellenbosch University, demonstrating that age is no barrier to lifelong learning. He...
